hero.label: Made for Corgi Insurance
hero.headline: I want to build the future of startup insurance at Corgi.
hero.subheadline: Corgi isn't just another insurance company, it's rebuilding the category from scratch with AI at its core. After closing $108M and winning regulatory approval, you're hiring at the exact moment when modern infrastructure meets massive market need. I've spent 18 months in B2B SaaS sales and saw firsthand how broken traditional insurance is for founders, and now I want to be part of fixing it.

whyCompany.title: AI-native insurance built for the startup era
whyCompany.body: Most insurance companies are 50 years old running on legacy systems. Corgi is different, building full-stack underwriting, claims, and operations on modern infrastructure from day one. That's not incremental improvement, that's category reinvention. I want to sell products that actually solve real startup problems instead of forcing founders into broken 20th century workflows.

whyCompany.title: Series A momentum with regulatory approval locked
whyCompany.body: You've just raised $108M and received regulatory approval to launch. This isn't a speculative hiring spree, this is the moment where an insurance company actually goes live with something fundamentally new. I'm joining at the inflection point where the product is real and the market is waiting.

whyCompany.title: BDR background built for this exact motion
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai, I cold outreached to SaaS founders and helped close 15-20 paying clients in a competitive market. At NextAfter, I learned how B2B fundraising consultancies actually work. Now Corgi needs BDRs to reach founders who desperately need modern coverage. I already know the buyer, their pain points, and how to reach them at scale.

quote: I spent a year watching founders get frustrated with insurance that doesn't fit their reality, and now Corgi is actually building something different. I want to be the person selling it to them.
